latest.title: Notice of Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 16:47
RNS Number : 3893L
Georgina Energy PLC
07 July 2026
07 July 2026
Georgina Energy Plc
(the "Company")
Notice of Annual General Meeting
Georgina Energy Plc, GEX.L, a helium, hydrogen and natural resources development company in Australia, announces that the Annual General Meeting ("AGM") of the Company will be held at 11 a.m. on 30 July 2026 at the offices of Howard Kennedy LLP, 1 London Bridge SE1 9BG.
The Notice of AGM, including a letter to shareholders from the Chairman, and Form of Proxy can be found on the Company's website at
https://www.georginaenergy.com/investors/shareholder-information/
.
The Company's annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 January 2026 is available on the Investors section of the Company's website at
https://www.georginaenergy.com/investors/results-reports-presentations/
.
Shareholders are requested to complete, sign and return the Form of Proxy to the Company's Registrars, Neville Registrars, at Neville House, Steelpark Road, Halesowen B62 8HD or by email to Neville Registrars at
info@nevilleregistrars.co.uk
.
The Form of Proxy must be returned as soon as possible but, in any event, so as to arrive no later than 11 a.m. on 28 July 2026.
END
Enquiries
Georgina Energy
Tony Hamilton
via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com
Mark Wallace
Tavira Financial Ltd - Financial Adviser and Joint Broker
Jonathan Evans
+44 (0)20 3833 3719
Oliver Stansfield
Financial PR via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com
Violet Wilson
+44 (0)203 757 4980
Georgia Edmonds
Notes to Editors
Georgina Energy aims to become a leading player in the global energy market and is focused on establishing itself among the top producers of helium and hydrogen worldwide. With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is located in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, which Georgina Energy will hold a 100% working interest on completion of the purchase agreement with Mosman Oil & Gas.
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter and EP513 Hussar projects for their potential accumulations.
For more information visit
https://www.georginaenergy.com

latest.title: Hussar EP513 Drill Pre-Drill Site Works Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7009K
Georgina Energy PLC
02 July 2026
2nd July 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Hussar EP513 Drill Pre-Drill Site Works Update
Georgina Energy Plc,
GEX.L
is pleased to confirm that contractors and personnel continue onsite undertaking the pre-drill site works required for the Q3 Hussar drilling program. Current operations are proceeding as planned
to enable the testing of this exciting prospect, with 300 km² of areal closure and
as
one of the largest subsalt Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbons prospects in onshore Australia.
Highlights
·
Pre-drill site works continue site at Hussar EP513, with current operations proceeding as planned.
·
Access, airstrip, water well, drill pad and accommodation pad works are being progressed ahead of rig mobilisation.
·
Contracted Ensign Rig 970 remains scheduled for mobilisation to site following completion of the current preparatory works, with an anticipated spud date in September 2026.
·
Hussar will target primary subsalt reservoir formations, including the Townsend Formation and fractured Neoproterozoic basement lithologies, for helium, hydrogen and natural gas.
GEX Personnel Inspecting Pre-Drill Site Works
The pre-drill site works are essential to be completed to ensure compliance with the Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration's approved Well Management Plan lodged by Georgina's wholly owned operating subsidiary Westmarket Oil & Gas Pty Ltd in 2025.
The Company's operations and technical consultancy teams have travelled and remain on site to ensure works on the road repairs, airstrip repairs, site access preparation, clear access for the water well drilling, preparation of the drill and accommodation pad are completed. The company is also working with the Traditional Owner's representatives to ensure compliance to its agreements and obligations.
Figure 1:
The Hussar Wellsite Plan
The contracted Ensign Rig 970 remains located in the North Perth Basin before being mobilised to site for Georgina, following completion of the current access, site, airstrip and water well drilling works pad and camp locations works, with an anticipated spud date in September 2026.
Hussar prospect lies in the Officer Basin, Western Australia in exploration permit EP513 (SPA 0036). Georgina Energy intends to drill the EP513 Hussar well to a depth of 3,200m, targeting the primary subsalt reservoir formations, including the Townsend Formation and the fractured Neoproterozoic basement lithologies, for helium, hydrogen and natural gas.
Independent consultants confirmed that Hussar has the required elements to yield net attributable 2U Prospective Resources of 155 BCFG Helium & 173 BCFG hydrogen with a potential combined in-situ value of US$55 billion (He US$350/MCFG, H US$2.65/kg) and net attributable 2U Prospective (recoverable) Resources of 1.73 TCFG of Natural Gas with potential in-situ value of US$5.24 billion
.
Site Inspections with Traditional Owners
GEX Pre-Drill Site Contractors
Hussar Airstrip rectification and repairs
Further updates will follow as we progress along with site photos which can be viewed by shareholders on the Georgina website.
Anthony Hamilton, Chief Executive Officer of Georgina Energy, commented:
"Georgina remains on target for the drilling and development of its wholly owned Hussar EP513 Project. We are pleased with the progress of current site works to ensure the Ensign Drill Rig will be mobilised and the current planning and civil engineering works will be successfully completed in order to enable the drill testing of this exciting prospect in Q3 2026. With 300 km2 of areal closure, the Hussar prospect is one of the largest subsalt Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbons prospects onshore in Australia."
ENDS
Enquiries
Georgina Energy
Tony Hamilton
via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com
Mark Wallace
Tavira Financial Ltd - Financial Adviser and Joint Broker
Jonathan Evans

+44 (0)203 757 4980
G
eorgia Edmonds
Notes to Editors
Georgina Energy aims to become a leading player in the global energy market and is focused on establishing itself among the top producers of helium and hydrogen worldwide. With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is located in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, subject to completion of the Sale Agreement Georgina Energy will hold a 100% working interest.
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter,
EP513 Hussar and the Mt Kitty and Dukas
projects for their potential accumulations.
For more information visit

latest.title: Mt Winter EPA 155 ALRA
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5453J
Georgina Energy PLC
24 June 2026
24th June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Mt Winter EPA 155 ALRA
Georgina Energy Plc,
GEX.L
confirms it has agreed the terms of the Aboriginal Land Rights Agreement (ALRA) with the Central Land Council (CLC) to facilitate the granting of EP155, which hosts the Mt Winter prospect. The Agreement will now be submitted to the Traditional Owners prior to execution then submission to the Minister of the Northern Territory Department of Mines and Energy for formal granting.
Highlights
·
Mt Winter is 70 kms west of the Mereenie Oil & Gas field which has been in production since 1984 and has produced over 16 million barrels of Oil, and 250 BCF of gas to date. Current annual production is 12 BCFG and 115,000 barrels of oil.
·
Mt Winter has Prospective Recoverable Resources at 2U/P50 of 283 BCF Helium, 214 BCF Hydrogen and 1,734 BCF Hydrocarbon Gas.
·
The Mt Winter prospect has an aerial closure exceeding 3,200 acres with exceptionally thick combined total targeted Heavitree and fractured basement reservoir sections.
·
The Amadeus Basin has demonstrated significant Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon flows with up to 9% Helium, 11.5% Hydrogen, and 40% hydrocarbon gas in the same subsalt reservoir section as targeted at Mt Winter.
The EP155 Mt Winter Permit location within significant oil and gas fields
Mt Winter EPA155
Under the terms of the ALRA the Company intends to expedite the prospect re-entry and development program for the highly prospective Mt Winter project along with its Hussar project in the Officer Basin which is currently being prepared for a drilling program. The Mt Winter permit EP155 has been under unsuccessful application for over 16 years when held by previous companies. Georgina will complete the settlement with Mosman Oil & Gas including the payment of
A
$300,000 (from current working capital) on granting by the Minister of the permit.
Anthony Hamilton, Chief Executive Officer of Georgina Energy, commented:
"The Company is very pleased with the outcome and wishes to thank Bob Liddle (OAM), our Indigenous Consultant and the Georgina team for their tireless work in consultation with the CLC and Traditional Owners in the negotiation and finalisation of the EP155 ALRA Agreement.
This is an important step toward the granting of EP155 and our planned re-entry and development programme at Mt Winter. The Company now has two active development workstreams, with Mt Winter progressing toward formal grant and Hussar advancing through pre-drill civil engineering works ahead of the planned Q3 2026 drilling programme."
END
Enquiries
Georgina Energy
Tony Hamilton
via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com

+44 (0)203 757 4980
Georgia Edmonds
Notes to Editors
Georgina Energy aims to become a leading player in the global energy market and is focused on establishing itself among the top producers of Helium and Hydrogen worldwide. With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, which Georgina Energy will hold a 100% interest on completion and granting by the Minister . The Amadeus Basin is known for Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon reservoirs with drilled occurrences of high concentrations of these gases (up to 9.5%, 11% and 40% respectively).
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter, EP513 Hussar for their potential accumulations.
For more information visit
https://www.georginaenergy.com

latest.title: Hussar EP513 Drill Mobilisation/Site Works
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6393I
Georgina Energy PLC
17 June 2026
17th June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Hussar EP513 Drill Mobilisation/Site Works
Georgina Energy Plc (LSE: GEX.L),
a helium and hydrogen exploration company with strategic permits in Australia,
is pleased to announce the payment, to Ensign Australia Pty Ltd, of the deposit for the supply of the Ensign 970 drill rig for the scheduled Q3 2026 drilling work at the 100% owned Hussar prospect EP513.
The Hussar prospect lies in the Officer Basin, Western Australia in exploration permit EP513 (SPA 0036). Georgina Energy intends to drill the EP513 Hussar well to a depth of 3,200m, targeting the primary subsalt reservoir formations, including the Townsend Formation and the fractured Neoproterozoic basement lithologies, for helium, hydrogen and natural gas.
Independent consultants confirmed that Hussar has the required elements to yield net attributable 2U Prospective Resources of 155 BCFG Helium & 173 BCFG hydrogen with a potential combined in-situ value of US$55 billion (He US$350/MCFG, H US$2.65/kg) and net attributable 2U Prospective (recoverable) Resources of 1.73 TCFG of Natural Gas with potential in-situ value of US$5.24 billion.
The drill testing timeline of Q3 2026 remains unchanged, and the drill program is planned for 50 days from spud to completion, subject to any changes deemed necessary as the program evolves.
Georgina's technical consultants Aztech Well Construction are working with suppliers for casing, wellhead, and service company support for electric logging, casing running and cementing, drilling fluid engineering, wellsite geology, mudlogging and gas sampling/laboratory analysis services. In addition, we are working with the traditional Owners to ensure compliance to all our obligations.
Contractors Renovating the Main Access Road
The pre-drill site inspections are important to confirm the work programs have been completed to ensure compliance with the Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration's approved Well Management Plan lodged by Georgina's wholly owned operating subsidiary Westmarket Oil & Gas Pty Ltd in 2025.
The Company's operations and technical consultancy teams will be travelling to site in this month of June 2026, to undertake inspections of the access road repairs, ensure site preparation, clear access for the water well drilling, preparation of the drill and accommodation pad.
Contractors Repairing Access roads along the Gunbarrel Highway.
The water bore drilling rig is expected to drill a maximum of two water bores on the Hussar site by the end of July 2026.
The contracted Ensign Rig 970 is currently located in the North Perth Basin before being mobilised to site for Georgina once the access, site, airstrip and water well drilling works have been completed.
The drilling program and site infrastructure works required to develop Hussar into a producing asset will be funded solely by Harlequin and their partners.
Anthony Hamilton, Chief Executive Officer of Georgina Energy, commented:
"Georgina is fully committed to the drilling and development of its wholly owned Hussar EP513 Project. We are pleased the deposit for Drill Rig Mobilisation for the supply of an Ensign Drill rig has been paid. The necessary preparatory planning and civil engineering works required have commenced in order to enable the drill testing of this exciting prospect in Q3 2026. With 300 km2 of a real closure, the Hussar prospect is one of the largest subsalt Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbons prospects on shore in Australia."
Enquiries
Georgina Energy
Tony Hamilton
via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com

With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, subject to completion of the Sale Agreement Georgina Energy will hold a 100% working interest and the Amadeus Basin is known for Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon reservoirs with drilled occurrences of high concentrations of these gases (up to 9.5%, 11% and 40% respectively).
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter, EP513 Hussar for their potential accumulations.

latest.title: Repayment of Debt
latest.content:
Today 10:28
RNS Number : 0265H
Georgina Energy PLC
04 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
4 June 2026
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Repayment of Debt
The Company has today paid the full the outstanding balance of $287,170.74 to extinguish a loan provided by CSS Alpha Fund ("CSS"). The loan was reported in our audited financial statements for the year ending 31 January 2026 ("FY26"). The current borrowings of the Company have now been reduced materially following this full repayment to CSS and the recent conversion of loans of approximately £293,000 by the provider of the debt facility as previously announced.
The Company's working capital position has strengthened materially following FY26 because of the recent capital raises and warrant exercise that have raised approximately £2.7m.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: GEX Ltd. supplies drilling rigs and services for on‑shore and off‑shore oil and gas projects. They build, lease and operate drilling equipment, and provide technical support to exploration companies.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global push for new energy resources keeps demand for drilling services high. GEX’s rigs are positioned to tap into emerging offshore fields, giving them a niche in a market that still relies heavily on traditional drilling infrastructure.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a capital‑raising phase, having just closed a £1 million fundraise and paid down debt. They are preparing to start a new drilling operation (Hussar EP513) and are working to meet environmental approvals. The stock is trading near zero, reflecting the heavy discount investors demand for a company that hasn’t yet proven the rig will be profitable.

rns_narrative: GEX has been busy shuffling its balance sheet: a fresh equity issue, a debt repayment, and a fundraise to finance the upcoming drill. These moves show the company is actively managing its cash and gearing up for a key operational milestone.

Operational updates – the drill site works are progressing and the company has received the necessary environmental approvals. The company also exercised warrants and terminated a share‑purchase agreement, signalling a shift in capital structure.
Corporate governance has been stable: a general meeting confirmed the board’s plans, and no red‑flag announcements (e.g., bankruptcy, delisting) have surfaced.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Everyone’s waiting for the drill to kick off – it’s the single event that could turn the stock upside.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are saying the drill is a ‘unbelievable opportunity’ and that the company’s valuation will soar once the rig starts producing. They keep urging people to hold tight and top up.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point out that the market is still pushing the price lower because the drill date is still uncertain, and daily falls suggest continued capitulation. They worry that if the rig fails to meet milestones, the price could collapse further.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from cautious optimism to a more hopeful tone as the drill‑site update arrives, but overall engagement remains low – the crowd is still on standby.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning no one is searching for GEX. A dead trend gives the best chance for a surprise rally when news hits.

near_term_catalysts: Official drill‑start announcement – the first day the rig is operational.
Positive drilling data: completion of the first well or a successful test of the rig’s capabilities.
A new contract or partnership that guarantees revenue for the rig.
A follow‑up capital raise that signals confidence from institutional investors.
A spike in trading volume that shows renewed interest after the drill announcement.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 2‑ to 3‑fold jump in daily volume, a price bounce that reaches the 200‑day moving average, and any press release confirming the rig’s operational status. If the drill stalls or the company issues a negative update, the upside evaporates.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is at the very bottom of a deep crash. If the drill starts on schedule, the mean‑reversion phase should begin immediately. If the drill is delayed or fails, the price may stay flat or drop further for months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is more than 70% below the last high, so a 50% recovery would more than double the current value. Historical deep‑crash returns (39% avg for green signals) show that once a catalyst hits, the upside can be 100–200% in a few months. The company’s recent capital moves and operational progress give a concrete reason for a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the drilling operation stalls or the rig fails to meet production targets, the price could slide to zero or trigger a delisting. The worst‑case scenario is a total loss of invested capital if the rig never becomes profitable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: +100% if the price rebounds to 50% of the pre‑crash peak. Potential downside: -100% if the drill fails. The asymmetry is clear – a single successful event could double the investment, while failure wipes it out.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed drill start or first well completion, buying at the nearest support (around the current price). Use a 10% position sizing rule and set a stop 20% below support. Hold until the price crosses the 200‑day moving average or a new positive announcement arrives.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of at least twice the 30‑day average and a price lift above the 50‑day moving average. If these appear before a drill‑start announcement, consider a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a material loss of drilling capability, a bankruptcy filing, or a delisting notice, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GEX is a deep‑crashed drilling company that is poised for a rebound once the new rig goes live – a single catalyst could double the price, but failure would wipe out the investment.
